Hey guys, JB here, talking to you with daily motivation slash skills, creating great one for today is about creating customers for life. What do you do after the prospect says yes? How do you take a one time buyer, turn them into a massive customer, right? How do you take someone who's just okay about your product? They love it, but they're not a fan. They're not like, you know, promoting on your behalf. Hey, it turns into a radiant fan who actually spreads the word, gives you referrals, right? And one of the stories that I tell in that training is about a guy who was trying to get into the Ford motor company. He was trying to sell them some parts, right? And he was after this one buyer at Ford motor and he was just asking for years and years and whatever he did, he couldn't get in the door. And the guy kept saying, listen, I'm happy with my current supplies. He's a nice guy, but he kept trying. He was trying to get everything, right? Took the guy out for lunch even, couldn't get it across. There's a $30, $40 million contract, right? Finally, when he's out for lunch with this buyer from Ford one day, he happened to casually start talking about their children and the subject of the buyer's son came up and he said that he loved this one sports team. And other than sports, he loved the quarterback. And like this quarterback was his idol, right? Well, just so happened is this guy, the salesperson knew how to get through to this quarterback and he had, let me get a signed ball from this quarterback to this kid's son and bam, let's see if that moves the needle enough to get this guy to give me a shot, right? Well, he does that and sure enough, bam, within a week, he gets this, a call from the Ford motor company, the buyer and the guy's like, I'm blown away. I can't get my whole life, nothing's ever like this happened. I have nothing for you right now, but I just thank you enough. Next time I really can hook you up, I definitely will. Well, guess what? Two weeks later, bam, he lands the contract $50 million a year because of a signed football. That's talking about giving ethical gifts correctly, right? Well, anyway, so one of the people on this program, great young kid, right? About 20 years old, he went to work for his dad's company and they had a small company they wanted to expand. The company is in a certain area of like insurance and reimbursements, right? And he was trying to get through to a massive, massive potential prospect, right? But couldn't move them off the mark. Whatever he did, he just couldn't get them past square one, right? Finally, he takes this tactic out of the straight line system, right? And he sends the very top person on the food line, the top five people in the company, the woman, he sends her a dozen roses or a few dozen roses, right? With a nice note saying, I'm sure I know I have some great people. Anyway, long story short, now this woman dictated, dictated a meeting at the highest level. He's got to show up at the company, have a presentation. They got a shot now to close this massive contract all because of these flowers. Without that, he was simply not getting through no way, no how was never going to happen. What does that mean for you? I'm telling you this. Well, sometimes when I tell you a story, you know, you don't really take it to heart what it really means to you, how you can apply it to your own life. This kid did and look at the results. But getting past the gatekeeper, right? There's a formula I never want you to forget. There's an inverse relationship based on how massive the client potentially is and how many people that you have to call. Now, if you have an unlimited number of prospects to call and this one particular one, well, yeah, they're okay. I wouldn't waste that much time trying to get past any one gatekeeper. But if you're in an industry where you have a limited number of people to call and one person could really triple the size of your business, you take out all the stops. You get it. You can use this strategy. It's so powerful by giving ethical gifts to people to very top or any level to get past the gatekeeper. I promise you, if you do this, it can change your business dramatically. It really can. Now, remember, what you don't want to do is spend a massive amount of time trying to get through gatekeepers when the payoff is very small. Even a closed sale is not going to move your needle of your business. You know what I'm saying? It's only for those situations where a payoff would have a dramatic impact on your company. It would really make a difference. And also, when you don't have an unlimited number of names to call. Otherwise, I recommend the slice and dice. Bam, bam. Tons of calls. Try to get past the gatekeeper by positioning yourself as a friend using the informal tonality and bam, that's your best strategy. But remember, using ethical gifts is unbelievably powerful for those big, high-level, long-sale cycle deals. All right? Don't forget that strategy. I'm on the beach ocean. It's out. It's beautiful, right? I worked at Everyday this week. Boom. I'm off the mark, right? So, I got so much stronger. I'm not a lot, but still, it's like I've totally, my muscles are waking up, feels great. And guys, don't forget this strategy. And because of that, they went by, but they'd pick up the phone a second time. One of the most frustrating things in sales, you know that, is getting through to a person the second time. So remember your ethical gifts, employ them correctly, meaning in cases where the payoff is very small, you're in a slice and dice, you got to make sure that the time and energy and the cost of the gift isn't enough to offset any profits or time loss doing it. And then when those rare occasions when there's just a mess of upside, then you have a different story. Go for the goal, all right? Love you all guys. Talk to you later.
